cow.title: Common Cow Diseases & Treatment
points: Foot-and-Mouth Disease (FMD): Symptoms include fever, blisters on mouth/feet. Vaccinate every 6 months. Isolate infected animals immediately.
Mastitis: Swollen udder, abnormal milk. Maintain udder hygiene, use teat dip after milking. Consult vet for antibiotics.
Bloat: Swollen left abdomen, difficulty breathing. Avoid sudden diet changes. Use anti-bloat oil. Emergency: use trocar for severe cases.
Brucellosis: Causes abortion, reduced milk. Test regularly (Rose Bengal test). Vaccinate calves at 4-8 months.
Black Quarter (BQ): Sudden lameness, swelling in muscles, high fever. Vaccinate annually. Requires immediate penicillin treatment.

buffalo.title: Common Buffalo Diseases & Treatment
points: Hemorrhagic Septicemia (HS): High fever, swollen throat, sudden death. Vaccinate before monsoon season.
Mastitis: Similar to cows. Clean milking practices are essential. Dry cow therapy recommended.
Parasitic infections: Regular deworming every 3 months. Use Ivermectin or Albendazole.
Foot Rot: Lameness, foul smell. Keep floors clean and dry. Copper sulfate foot bath helps.
Theileriosis: Tick-borne, causes fever and anemia. Use acaricides for tick control.

goat.title: Common Goat Diseases & Treatment
points: PPR (Peste des Petits Ruminants): Fever, nasal discharge, diarrhea. Vaccinate once a year.
Enterotoxemia: Sudden death, bloat. Vaccinate kids at 4 months, booster every 6 months.
Foot Rot: Trim hooves regularly, use zinc sulfate foot bath. Keep pens dry.
Pneumonia: Coughing, nasal discharge. Ensure proper ventilation. Antibiotics needed.
Internal Parasites: Deworm every 2-3 months. Use FAMACHA scoring for anemia checks.

sheep.title: Common Sheep Diseases & Treatment
points: Blue Tongue: Swollen tongue, drooling, fever. Vaccinate before monsoon. No direct treatment.
Sheep Pox: Fever, skin nodules, scabs. Vaccinate annually. Isolate infected sheep.
Liver Fluke: Weight loss, bottle jaw. Deworm with Triclabendazole. Avoid wet grazing areas.
Wool Slip / Fleece Rot: Caused by moisture. Ensure proper shearing and dry housing.
Foot Rot: Regular hoof trimming. Use 10% zinc sulfate foot bath weekly.

poultry.title: Common Poultry Diseases & Treatment
points: Newcastle Disease (Ranikhet): Respiratory distress, green droppings. Vaccinate at day 7, 21, and every 3 months.
Fowl Pox: Scabs on comb/wattle. Vaccinate at 6 weeks. No direct cure once infected.
Coccidiosis: Bloody droppings, weakness. Use anticoccidial drugs (Amprolium). Keep litter dry.
Infectious Bursal Disease (IBD): Feather ruffling, white diarrhea. Vaccinate at 14-18 days.
Avian Influenza: Sudden death, swollen head. Report immediately. Practice strict biosecurity.

cow.title: Cow Feeding Guidelines
points: Daily Dry Matter: Feed 2.5-3% of body weight. A 400kg cow needs 10-12 kg dry matter/day.
Green Fodder: 25-30 kg daily (Napier grass, Lucerne, Maize). Chop before feeding.
Dry Fodder: 5-6 kg daily (paddy straw, wheat straw). Treat with urea (4%) to improve digestibility.
Concentrate Feed: 1 kg per 2.5 litres of milk produced. Mix grain, oilcake, bran, minerals.
Water: Provide 40-60 litres of clean water daily. More during summers and lactation.
Mineral Mix: 50-60g daily. Essential for milk production and bone health.

buffalo.title: Buffalo Feeding Guidelines
points: Green Fodder: 30-40 kg daily. Prefer Berseem, Lucerne, or Napier grass.
Dry Fodder: 5-8 kg daily. Paddy straw or wheat bhusa. Urea treatment improves quality.
Concentrate: 1 kg per 2 litres of milk. Higher protein needed (18-20% CP).
During Pregnancy: Extra 1.5 kg concentrate in last 2 months. Add bypass fat.
Water: 50-70 litres daily. Buffaloes need wallowing or sprinklers in summer.
Tip: Feed concentrates before milking for better letdown.

goat.title: Goat Feeding Guidelines
points: Grazing: Allow 6-8 hours of browsing daily. Goats prefer shrubs and tree leaves.
Dry Fodder: 1-2 kg daily (hay, groundnut haulms). Provide in evenings.
Concentrate: 200-300g daily for milking does. Use goat-specific mineral mix.
Kidding Period: Increase feed by 50% in late pregnancy. Colostrum within 1 hour of birth.
Water: 4-5 litres daily. Always keep clean water accessible.
Avoid: Azalea, rhododendron, and oleander leaves — toxic to goats.

sheep.title: Sheep Feeding Guidelines
points: Grazing: 8 hours daily on natural pastures. Rotational grazing recommended.
Hay: 1-2 kg per day when pasture is limited. Legume hay is ideal.
Grain: 200-400g daily for ewes. Increase during flushing (pre-breeding).
Minerals: Provide salt lick. Sheep need selenium and copper supplements.
Lamb Feeding: Creep feeding from 2 weeks old. Start with 50g, increase gradually.
Water: 4-8 litres per day. Lactating ewes need more.

poultry.title: Poultry Feeding Guidelines
points: Layer Feed: 110-120g per bird/day. 16-18% protein with 3.5% calcium.
Broiler Feed: Starter (0-2 wk): 22% protein. Grower (2-4 wk): 20%. Finisher (4-6 wk): 18%.
Water: 250-300 ml per bird/day. Add electrolytes during summer.
Grit: Provide insoluble grit for digestion. Oyster shell for calcium.
Feed Conversion: Target FCR of 1.6-1.8 for broilers. Monitor weekly weight gain.
Storage: Keep feed dry and pest-free. Use within 2 weeks of manufacturing.

cow.title: Cow Maintenance & Care Guide
points: Housing: 40 sq.ft per cow. Good ventilation, clean floor. Slope for drainage.
Daily Routine: Milking 2x/day at fixed times. Brush coat daily. Check hooves weekly.
Vaccination Schedule: FMD (every 6 mo), BQ (annually), Brucella (calf 4-8 mo), HS (pre-monsoon).
Deworming: Every 3 months with broad-spectrum anthelmintic. Rotate drug classes.
Heat Detection: Watch for mounting, mucus discharge, restlessness. AI within 12-18 hours.
Record Keeping: Maintain breeding, health, milk production records for each animal.

buffalo.title: Buffalo Maintenance & Care Guide
points: Housing: 50 sq.ft per buffalo. Buffer need mud/water wallowing area in summer.
Heat Management: Sprinklers, fans, or wallowing pond essential in hot weather.
Milking: Twice daily. Complete milk letdown important for buffalo. Use oxytocin only under vet guidance.
Hoof Care: Trim every 3-4 months. Check for foot rot regularly.
Breeding: Buffaloes show silent heat. Use teaser bull or progesterone assay.
Calf Rearing: Allow suckling for 2-3 days. Wean gradually. Vaccinate as per schedule.

goat.title: Goat Maintenance & Care Guide
points: Housing: Elevated slatted floor preferred. 10-15 sq.ft per goat. Good ventilation.
Hygiene: Clean pens daily. Disinfect monthly with lime water.
Hoof Trimming: Every 6-8 weeks. Prevents foot rot and lameness.
Vaccination: PPR (annually), Enterotoxemia (every 6 months), Goat Pox (annually).
Breeding: Buck:doe ratio 1:25-30. Breeding season: Aug-Dec in most regions.
Kid Care: Navel dipping with iodine at birth. Colostrum within first hour.

sheep.title: Sheep Maintenance & Care Guide
points: Shearing: Twice a year (before summer and winter). Use clean, sharp shears.
Housing: 10-15 sq.ft per sheep. Protect from rain, predators, and extreme heat.
Dipping: Dip in acaricide solution every 3 months to control external parasites.
Foot Care: Regular trimming. Foot bath with zinc sulfate every 2 weeks.
Breeding: Flushing 2 weeks before mating. Ram:ewe ratio 1:30-40.
Lambing: Clean, dry pen. Assist only if needed. Navel care essential.

poultry.title: Poultry Maintenance & Care Guide
points: Housing: 2 sq.ft per bird (deep litter). Good ventilation. Protect from predators.
Litter: 3-4 inch thick rice husk or wood shavings. Turn daily. Replace when wet.
Lighting: 16 hours light for layers. Use timer-controlled CFL/LED bulbs.
Biosecurity: Foot bath at entry. Limit visitors. All-in-all-out system preferred.
Vaccination: Newcastle (Day 7, 21), IBD (Day 14), Fowl Pox (Week 6), Deworming (monthly).
Egg Collection: Collect 3-4 times daily. Store at cool temperature. Grade before marketing.

cow.title: Cow Breeding Tips
points: Age at First Breeding: 15-18 months or when body weight reaches 250-275 kg.
Heat Signs: Mucus discharge, mounting behavior, reduced milk, swollen vulva.
AI Timing: Inseminate 12-18 hours after standing heat detected (AM-PM rule).
Pregnancy Check: Rectal palpation at 60 days. Or use pregnancy kits at 28 days.
Dry Period: Stop milking 60 days before expected calving. Increase concentrate.
Post-Calving: Watch for retained placenta. Resume breeding 60-90 days after calving.

buffalo.title: Buffalo Breeding Tips
points: Silent Heat: Use teaser bull or tail paint method for detection.
AI Timing: Best results in evening/night insemination.
Calving Interval: Target 13-15 months. Supplementary feeding improves conception.
Seasonal Breeding: Buffaloes breed better in short day length (Sept-Feb).
Pregnancy: Gestation ~310 days. Provide extra care in last trimester.

goat.title: Goat Breeding Tips
points: Breeding Age: Does at 10-12 months (25-30 kg). Bucks at 12 months.
Flushing: Increase feed 2-3 weeks before breeding to boost ovulation.
Gestation: ~150 days. Twins are common in well-managed herds.
Buck Selection: Choose for growth rate, disease resistance, and twinning trait.
Post-Kidding: Allow doe to lick kids. Ensure colostrum within 1 hour.

sheep.title: Sheep Breeding Tips
points: Breeding Season: Aug-Dec typically. Use ram effect for synchronization.
Ewes: Breed at 12-14 months. Flush 2 weeks before mating.
Ram Management: Check fertility 6 weeks before season. Balanced nutrition essential.
Gestation: ~147 days. Scan at 60-90 days to identify multiples.
Lambing: Prepare clean pens. Assist only when necessary.

poultry.title: Poultry Breeding Tips
points: Cock:Hen Ratio: 1:10 for heavy breeds, 1:12 for light breeds.
Breeder Age: Start at 24-26 weeks. Peak fertility at 30-40 weeks.
Egg Selection: Medium size, clean, no cracks. Store pointed end down.
Incubation: 21 days at 37.5°C, 60% humidity. Turn eggs 3-5 times daily.
Hatching: Increase humidity to 70% on day 19. Do not open incubator.

cow.title: Cow Vaccination Schedule
points: FMD: First at 4 months, booster at 9 months, then every 6 months.
Black Quarter: First at 6 months, then annually before monsoon.
HS: Annually before monsoon season.
Brucella: Female calves at 4-8 months (one time only — S19 vaccine).
Theileriosis: Endemic areas — vaccinate at 3 months with live vaccine.
Deworming: Every 3 months. Alternate Albendazole and Fenbendazole.

buffalo.title: Buffalo Vaccination Schedule
points: HS: Most critical — annually before monsoon.
FMD: Every 6 months starting at 4 months of age.
BQ: Annually before monsoon.
Brucella: Female calves at 4-8 months.
Deworming: Every 3-4 months. Include liver fluke treatment.

goat.title: Goat Vaccination Schedule
points: PPR: Once at 3 months, provides lifelong immunity.
Enterotoxemia: First at 4 months, booster in 15 days, then every 6 months.
Goat Pox: At 3 months, then annually.
FMD: Every 6 months in endemic areas.
Deworming: Every 2-3 months. Use FAMACHA scoring.

sheep.title: Sheep Vaccination Schedule
points: Enterotoxemia: At 4 months, booster in 15 days, then every 6 months.
Sheep Pox: At 3 months, then annually.
PPR: Once at 3 months — lifelong immunity.
Blue Tongue: Before monsoon in endemic areas.
Deworming: Monthly during monsoon, quarterly otherwise.

poultry.title: Poultry Vaccination Schedule
points: Day 1: Marek's Disease (at hatchery).
Day 7: Newcastle Disease (Lasota — eye drop/drinking water).
Day 14: IBD (Gumboro) — drinking water.
Day 21: Newcastle booster.
Week 6: Fowl Pox — wing web method.
Week 8: Newcastle (R2B — injection).

cow.title: Improving Cow Milk Production
points: Feeding: 1 kg concentrate per 2.5 litres of milk. Include bypass fat (100-150g/day).
Milking Technique: Full-hand milking preferred. Complete milking within 7 minutes.
Frequency: 3x milking can increase yield by 10-15%. Maintain consistent timing.
Udder Health: Pre-milking teat dip, strip first 2-3 streams. Post-milking teat dip.
Comfort: Clean, ventilated housing. Reduce stress. Maintain calm environment.
Genetics: Use proven AI sires. Cross-breeding with HF/Jersey improves yield.

buffalo.title: Improving Buffalo Milk Production
points: Cooling: Buffaloes produce more milk when cool. Provide sprinklers/wallowing.
Feeding: 1 kg concentrate per 2 litres milk. Add bypass protein.
Oxytocin: Avoid dependency. Train for calf-less letdown with proper conditioning.
Fat Content: Buffalo milk has 6-8% fat. Feed quality affects fat percentage.
Selection: Choose Murrah, Nili-Ravi, or Jaffarabadi for high yield.

goat.title: Goat Milk Production Tips
points: Breed Selection: Saanen, Alpine, Jamunapari for milk. Choose proven milking does.
Feeding: Quality legume hay + 300-500g concentrate for milking does.
Milking: Twice daily. Machine milking possible for larger herds.
Hygiene: Clean udder before milking. Cool milk immediately.
Market: Goat milk demand growing for health-conscious consumers. Premium pricing.

sheep.title: Sheep Milk Info
points: Note: Sheep milk is less common commercially but very rich.
Fat Content: 7-8% fat, higher than cow/goat milk.
Uses: Ideal for cheese (Roquefort, Feta, Pecorino).
Breeds: East Friesian, Lacaune are top dairy sheep breeds.

poultry.title: Egg Production Tips
points: Layer Breeds: White Leghorn (300+ eggs/yr), BV-380, Hy-Line.
Lighting: 16 hours of light daily triggers egg production.
Calcium: 3.5-4% calcium in diet. Oyster shell grit as supplement.
Nesting: 1 nest box per 4-5 hens. Keep nests dark and clean.
Stress: Reduce noise, overcrowding, temperature extremes for consistent laying.
Peak Production: 26-34 weeks. Expect 85-95% production rate.
